chunk.content: # Prometheus Alerting and Recording Rules

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_alerting_rules.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Prometheus Alerting and Recording Rules

Content:
# Prometheus Alerting and Recording Rules

chunk.content:
## Purpose
This document explains how Prometheus evaluates recording rules and alerting rules.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_alerting_rules.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Purpose

Content:
## Purpose
This document explains how Prometheus evaluates recording rules and alerting rules.

chunk.content:
## Overview
Prometheus periodically evaluates rule groups. A recording rule precomputes a PromQL expression and stores the result as a new time series. An alerting rule evaluates a condition and tracks alert state.

Rules are evaluated using PromQL. If an expression fails due to invalid syntax, duplicate series matching, or many-to-many vector matching, the rule state may not update successfully.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_alerting_rules.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Overview

Content:
## Overview
Prometheus periodically evaluates rule groups. A recording rule precomputes a PromQL expression and stores the result as a new time series. An alerting rule evaluates a condition and tracks alert state.

Rules are evaluated using PromQL. If an expression fails due to invalid syntax, duplicate series matching, or many-to-many vector matching, the rule state may not update successfully.

chunk.content:
## Key Components
- Rule Manager: Schedules and evaluates rule groups.
- Rule Group: A set of rules evaluated at a fixed interval.
- Recording Rule: Writes expression results as time series.
- Alerting Rule: Creates or updates alerts based on expression results.
- PromQL Engine: Executes rule expressions.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_alerting_rules.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Key Components

Content:
## Key Components
- Rule Manager: Schedules and evaluates rule groups.
- Rule Group: A set of rules evaluated at a fixed interval.
- Recording Rule: Writes expression results as time series.
- Alerting Rule: Creates or updates alerts based on expression results.
- PromQL Engine: Executes rule expressions.

chunk.content:
## Flow
1. Prometheus loads rule files from configuration.
2. Rule manager starts rule groups.
3. Each group is evaluated at its configured interval.
4. PromQL expression is executed.
5. Recording rule output is written to TSDB.
6. Alerting rule state is updated.
7. Active alerts are sent to Alertmanager if configured.

4. PromQL expression is executed.
5. Recording rule output is written to TSDB.
6. Alerting rule state is updated.
7. Active alerts are sent to Alertmanager if configured.

chunk.content:
## Common Failures
- Invalid PromQL syntax.
- Many-to-many matching error.
- Duplicate series for matching labels.
- Query timeout during rule evaluation.
- Rule file reload failure.
- High-cardinality query causing slow evaluation.

chunk.content: # Prometheus Scrape Lifecycle

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_scrape_lifecycle.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Prometheus Scrape Lifecycle

Content:
# Prometheus Scrape Lifecycle

chunk.content:
## Purpose
This document explains how Prometheus collects metrics from configured or discovered targets.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_scrape_lifecycle.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Purpose

Content:
## Purpose
This document explains how Prometheus collects metrics from configured or discovered targets.

chunk.content:
## Overview
Prometheus follows a pull-based model. Instead of applications pushing metrics directly to Prometheus, Prometheus periodically sends HTTP requests to target endpoints and scrapes metrics from them.

A scrape job is configured through `scrape_config`. Each scrape job defines which targets to scrape, how frequently to scrape them, and how to apply labels or relabeling rules. Targets may be configured statically or discovered dynamically using service discovery.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_scrape_lifecycle.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Overview

Content:
## Overview
Prometheus follows a pull-based model. Instead of applications pushing metrics directly to Prometheus, Prometheus periodically sends HTTP requests to target endpoints and scrapes metrics from them.

A scrape job is configured through `scrape_config`. Each scrape job defines which targets to scrape, how frequently to scrape them, and how to apply labels or relabeling rules. Targets may be configured statically or discovered dynamically using service discovery.

chunk.content:
## Key Components
- Scrape Manager: Coordinates scrape pools.
- Scrape Pool: Represents a group of targets for a job.
- Target: A metrics endpoint, usually exposed over HTTP.
- Service Discovery: Provides target groups dynamically.
- Relabeling: Rewrites or filters target labels before scraping.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_scrape_lifecycle.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Key Components

Content:
## Key Components
- Scrape Manager: Coordinates scrape pools.
- Scrape Pool: Represents a group of targets for a job.
- Target: A metrics endpoint, usually exposed over HTTP.
- Service Discovery: Provides target groups dynamically.
- Relabeling: Rewrites or filters target labels before scraping.

chunk.content:
## Flow
1. Prometheus loads the configuration.
2. Service discovery finds target groups.
3. Relabeling transforms discovered labels.
4. Scrape pools are created or updated.
5. Prometheus periodically sends HTTP requests to each target's metrics endpoint.
6. Scraped samples are parsed and appended to local TSDB.
7. Failed scrapes mark the target as down and update the `up` metric.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_scrape_lifecycle.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Flow

Content:
## Flow
1. Prometheus loads the configuration.
2. Service discovery finds target groups.
3. Relabeling transforms discovered labels.
4. Scrape pools are created or updated.
5. Prometheus periodically sends HTTP requests to each target's metrics endpoint.
6. Scraped samples are parsed and appended to local TSDB.
7. Failed scrapes mark the target as down and update the `up` metric.

chunk.content:
## Common Failures
- Target endpoint is unreachable.
- Scrape request times out.
- Target returns HTTP 500 or 503.
- Metrics format is invalid.
- Relabeling drops the target unexpectedly.
- Scrape interval or timeout is misconfigured.

chunk.content: # Prometheus Remote Write

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_remote_write.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Prometheus Remote Write

Content:
# Prometheus Remote Write

chunk.content:
## Purpose
This document explains how Prometheus sends samples to remote storage.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_remote_write.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Purpose

Content:
## Purpose
This document explains how Prometheus sends samples to remote storage.

chunk.content:
## Overview
Remote write allows Prometheus to send scraped samples to a remote system for long-term storage or centralized aggregation. Prometheus still scrapes targets locally, then sends samples asynchronously to configured remote write endpoints.

Remote write usually uses queues and batching. If the remote endpoint is slow or unavailable, queues can grow. If queues reach capacity, samples may be dropped.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_remote_write.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Overview

Content:
## Overview
Remote write allows Prometheus to send scraped samples to a remote system for long-term storage or centralized aggregation. Prometheus still scrapes targets locally, then sends samples asynchronously to configured remote write endpoints.

Remote write usually uses queues and batching. If the remote endpoint is slow or unavailable, queues can grow. If queues reach capacity, samples may be dropped.

chunk.content:
## Key Components
- Remote Write Queue Manager: Manages outgoing samples.
- Shards: Parallel workers sending batches.
- Remote Endpoint: Receiver for remote write data.
- Retry Logic: Handles temporary send failures.
- Backpressure Handling: Limits memory usage when remote endpoint is slow.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_remote_write.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Key Components

Content:
## Key Components
- Remote Write Queue Manager: Manages outgoing samples.
- Shards: Parallel workers sending batches.
- Remote Endpoint: Receiver for remote write data.
- Retry Logic: Handles temporary send failures.
- Backpressure Handling: Limits memory usage when remote endpoint is slow.

chunk.content:
## Flow
1. Prometheus scrapes targets.
2. Samples are appended to local TSDB.
3. Samples are sent to remote write queues.
4. Queue shards send batches to remote endpoint.
5. Failed sends are retried.
6. If the queue fills, samples may be dropped.

4. Queue shards send batches to remote endpoint.
5. Failed sends are retried.
6. If the queue fills, samples may be dropped.

chunk.content:
## Common Failures
- Remote endpoint unavailable.
- HTTP 429 rate limiting.
- HTTP 500 from remote storage.
- Queue capacity exceeded.
- Network timeout.
- High-cardinality metrics overloading remote storage.

chunk.content: # Prometheus TSDB Storage

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_tsdb_storage.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Prometheus TSDB Storage

Content:
# Prometheus TSDB Storage

chunk.content:
## Purpose
This document explains Prometheus local storage at a high level.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_tsdb_storage.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Purpose

Content:
## Purpose
This document explains Prometheus local storage at a high level.

chunk.content:
## Overview
Prometheus stores scraped samples in a local time-series database called TSDB. The storage layer is responsible for appending samples, maintaining write-ahead logs, compacting blocks, and serving queries.

Local storage is optimized for time-series workloads. Samples are first written to the WAL for durability and later compacted into blocks.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_tsdb_storage.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Overview

Content:
## Overview
Prometheus stores scraped samples in a local time-series database called TSDB. The storage layer is responsible for appending samples, maintaining write-ahead logs, compacting blocks, and serving queries.

Local storage is optimized for time-series workloads. Samples are first written to the WAL for durability and later compacted into blocks.

chunk.content:
## Key Components
- Head Block: In-memory and recent samples.
- WAL: Write-ahead log used for durability.
- Blocks: Persisted chunks and indexes for historical data.
- Compaction: Merges smaller blocks into larger blocks.
- Retention: Removes old blocks based on time or size limits.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_tsdb_storage.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Key Components

Content:
## Key Components
- Head Block: In-memory and recent samples.
- WAL: Write-ahead log used for durability.
- Blocks: Persisted chunks and indexes for historical data.
- Compaction: Merges smaller blocks into larger blocks.
- Retention: Removes old blocks based on time or size limits.

chunk.content:
## Flow
1. Scrape manager collects samples.
2. Samples are appended to TSDB head.
3. WAL records sample data for recovery.
4. Periodically, head data is cut into persistent blocks.
5. Compaction merges blocks.
6. Queries read from head and persisted blocks.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_tsdb_storage.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Flow

Content:
## Flow
1. Scrape manager collects samples.
2. Samples are appended to TSDB head.
3. WAL records sample data for recovery.
4. Periodically, head data is cut into persistent blocks.
5. Compaction merges blocks.
6. Queries read from head and persisted blocks.

chunk.content:
## Common Failures
- WAL corruption.
- Disk full.
- Permission issue on storage path.
- Slow disk causing ingestion lag.
- Excessive cardinality causing high memory usage.
- Retention misconfiguration.

chunk.content: # Prometheus Service Discovery

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_service_discovery.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Prometheus Service Discovery

Content:
# Prometheus Service Discovery

chunk.content:
## Purpose
This document explains how Prometheus discovers scrape targets dynamically.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_service_discovery.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Purpose

Content:
## Purpose
This document explains how Prometheus discovers scrape targets dynamically.

chunk.content:
## Overview
In dynamic environments such as Kubernetes, targets appear and disappear frequently. Prometheus supports service discovery so that scrape targets do not always need to be listed statically.

A service discovery provider returns target groups. These target groups contain labels describing discovered objects. Prometheus then applies relabeling rules to decide which targets to keep, drop, or transform.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_service_discovery.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Overview

Content:
## Overview
In dynamic environments such as Kubernetes, targets appear and disappear frequently. Prometheus supports service discovery so that scrape targets do not always need to be listed statically.

A service discovery provider returns target groups. These target groups contain labels describing discovered objects. Prometheus then applies relabeling rules to decide which targets to keep, drop, or transform.

chunk.content:
## Flow
1. Prometheus starts service discovery providers based on configuration.
2. Providers watch infrastructure APIs such as Kubernetes.
3. Discovered target groups are sent to the scrape manager.
4. Relabeling rules transform metadata labels into scrape labels.
5. Final targets are assigned to scrape pools.
6. Scrape manager updates active targets.

metadata.description: Self-written mini design docs for Prometheus operational concepts

searchable_text:
Source Type: design_doc
Path: data/design_docs/prometheus_service_discovery.md
Source ID: design_docs
Section: Flow

Content:
## Flow
1. Prometheus starts service discovery providers based on configuration.
2. Providers watch infrastructure APIs such as Kubernetes.
3. Discovered target groups are sent to the scrape manager.
4. Relabeling rules transform metadata labels into scrape labels.
5. Final targets are assigned to scrape pools.
6. Scrape manager updates active targets.

chunk.content:
## Kubernetes Example
For Kubernetes pod discovery, Prometheus may need permission to list and watch pods. If RBAC permissions are missing, service discovery can fail and targets will not be scraped.

## Kubernetes Example
For Kubernetes pod discovery, Prometheus may need permission to list and watch pods. If RBAC permissions are missing, service discovery can fail and targets will not be scraped.

chunk.content:
## Common Failures
- Missing RBAC permission.
- API server unavailable.
- Wrong namespace selector.
- Relabeling rule drops all discovered targets.
- Incorrect port or scheme label.
- High churn causing frequent target updates.

Source ID: incidents

chunk.content:

The corrupted WAL segment was isolated, storage repair was performed, and Prometheus was restarted.

metadata.description: Synthetic incident reports based on common Prometheus failures

searchable_text:
Source Type: incident

Source ID: incidents
Section: Resolution

Content:
## Resolution
The corrupted WAL segment was isolated, storage repair was performed, and Prometheus was restarted.

chunk.content:
## Preventive Actions
- Ensure graceful shutdown.
- Use reliable persistent volumes.
- Monitor disk health and filesystem errors.
- Keep remote write or backup strategy for critical metrics.

Prometheus failed to discover Kubernetes pod targets because the service account lacked permission to list pods.

## Summary
Prometheus failed to discover Kubernetes pod targets because the service account lacked permission to list pods.

chunk.content:
## User Impact
New pod targets were not discovered and therefore were not scraped.

metadata.description: Synthetic incident reports based on common Prometheus failures

searchable_text:
Source Type: incident
Path: data/incidents/INC-006-service-discovery-failure.md
Source ID: incidents
Section: User Impact

Content:
## User Impact
New pod targets were not discovered and therefore were not scraped.

chunk.content:
## Symptoms
- Kubernetes service discovery sync failed.
- Logs showed RBAC permission denied.
- Error mentioned service account `system:serviceaccount:monitoring:prometheus`.
- Pod targets were missing from the target list.

metadata.description: Synthetic incident reports based on common Prometheus failures

searchable_text:
Source Type: incident
Path: data/incidents/INC-006-service-discovery-failure.md
Source ID: incidents
Section: Symptoms

Content:
## Symptoms
- Kubernetes service discovery sync failed.
- Logs showed RBAC permission denied.
- Error mentioned service account `system:serviceaccount:monitoring:prometheus`.
- Pod targets were missing from the target list.

chunk.content:
## Timeline
- 08:45 UTC: Kubernetes service discovery started.
- 08:45 UTC: First RBAC failure observed.
- 08:46 UTC: Retry attempted.
- 08:47 UTC: Discovery sync failed again.
- 09:00 UTC: ClusterRole permissions were corrected.

metadata.description: Synthetic incident reports based on common Prometheus failures

searchable_text:
Source Type: incident
Path: data/incidents/INC-006-service-discovery-failure.md
Source ID: incidents
Section: Timeline

Content:
## Timeline
- 08:45 UTC: Kubernetes service discovery started.
- 08:45 UTC: First RBAC failure observed.
- 08:46 UTC: Retry attempted.
- 08:47 UTC: Discovery sync failed again.
- 09:00 UTC: ClusterRole permissions were corrected.

chunk.content:
## Root Cause
The Prometheus service account did not have permission to list pod resources at cluster scope.

metadata.description: Synthetic incident reports based on common Prometheus failures

searchable_text:
Source Type: incident
Path: data/incidents/INC-006-service-discovery-failure.md
Source ID: incidents
Section: Root Cause

Content:
## Root Cause
The Prometheus service account did not have permission to list pod resources at cluster scope.

chunk.content:
## Resolution
Updated Kubernetes RBAC rules to allow the Prometheus service account to list and watch pods.

metadata.description: Synthetic incident reports based on common Prometheus failures

searchable_text:
Source Type: incident
Path: data/incidents/INC-006-service-discovery-failure.md
Source ID: incidents
Section: Resolution

Content:
## Resolution
Updated Kubernetes RBAC rules to allow the Prometheus service account to list and watch pods.

chunk.content:
## Preventive Actions
- Validate RBAC during deployment.
- Add smoke test for service discovery.
- Monitor discovered target count.
- Alert when target count drops unexpectedly.

metadata.description: Synthetic incident reports based on common Prometheus failures

searchable_text:
Source Type: incident

Source ID: incidents
Section: Root Cause

Content:
## Root Cause
A malformed indentation in `prometheus.yml` caused YAML parsing failure.

chunk.content:
## Resolution
Fixed the YAML indentation and validated the configuration using promtool before reloading.

metadata.description: Synthetic incident reports based on common Prometheus failures

searchable_text:
Source Type: incident
Path: data/incidents/INC-002-config-reload-failure.md
Source ID: incidents
Section: Resolution

Content:
## Resolution
Fixed the YAML indentation and validated the configuration using promtool before reloading.

chunk.content:
## Preventive Actions
- Validate config in CI before deployment.
- Run `promtool check config` before reload.
- Add pre-commit YAML linting.
- Keep previous valid config as fallback.

Prometheus remote write fell behind because the remote endpoint returned HTTP 429 responses. The queue reached capacity and samples were dropped.

Downstream long-term storage missed samples. Some historical dashboards showed gaps.

metadata.description: Synthetic incident reports based on common Prometheus failures

searchable_text:
Source Type: incident

Source ID: incidents

Content:
## User Impact
Downstream long-term storage missed samples. Some historical dashboards showed gaps.

chunk.content:
## Symptoms

The `HighErrorRate` alert rule failed because its PromQL expression produced duplicate matching series and many-to-many matching was not allowed.

The alert state was not updated, so high error rate conditions could have been missed.

- 11:01 UTC: First evaluation failure.
- 11:02 UTC: Repeated many-to-many matching error.
- 11:15 UTC: Rule expression was corrected.

chunk.content:
## Root Cause
The alert expression joined two metrics without ensuring unique labels on one side of the match.

metadata.description: Synthetic incident reports based on common Prometheus failures

searchable_text:
Source Type: incident
Path: data/incidents/INC-005-alert-rule-evaluation-failure.md
Source ID: incidents
Section: Root Cause

Content:
## Root Cause
The alert expression joined two metrics without ensuring unique labels on one side of the match.

chunk.content:
## Resolution
The PromQL expression was fixed by aggregating the right-hand side metric before the join.

metadata.description: Synthetic incident reports based on common Prometheus failures

searchable_text:
Source Type: incident
Path: data/incidents/INC-005-alert-rule-evaluation-failure.md
Source ID: incidents
Section: Resolution

Content:
## Resolution
The PromQL expression was fixed by aggregating the right-hand side metric before the join.

chunk.content:
## Preventive Actions
- Add promtool validation in CI.
- Add rule tests for alert expressions.
- Review label cardinality before vector matching.
- Use recording rules for complex expressions.

Content:
## Summary
Prometheus marked a Kubernetes pod target as down after repeated scrape failures. The target endpoint returned timeout errors and intermittent HTTP 503 responses.

chunk.content:
## User Impact
Metrics from the affected pod were missing, causing dashboards and alerts for that pod to become unreliable.

Metrics from the affected pod were missing, causing dashboards and alerts for that pod to become unreliable.

- Target `http://10.42.1.15:9100/metrics` showed as down.
- Logs showed `context deadline exceeded`.
- Logs also showed HTTP 503 responses from the metrics endpoint.

Source Type: incident

Content:
## Timeline
- 10:15 UTC: Scrape manager started.
- 10:16 UTC: First timeout observed.
- 10:17 UTC: Repeated timeout observed.
- 10:18 UTC: Target marked down.

chunk.content:
## Root Cause
The target application was overloaded and could not respond to Prometheus scrape requests within the configured scrape timeout.

metadata.description: Synthetic incident reports based on common Prometheus failures

searchable_text:
Source Type: incident
Path: data/incidents/INC-001-scrape-timeout.md
Source ID: incidents
Section: Root Cause

Content:
## Root Cause
The target application was overloaded and could not respond to Prometheus scrape requests within the configured scrape timeout.

chunk.content:
## Resolution
The application was restarted and scrape timeout was temporarily increased for the affected job.

metadata.description: Synthetic incident reports based on common Prometheus failures

searchable_text:
Source Type: incident
Path: data/incidents/INC-001-scrape-timeout.md
Source ID: incidents
Section: Resolution

Content:
## Resolution
The application was restarted and scrape timeout was temporarily increased for the affected job.

chunk.content:
## Preventive Actions
- Add alert for repeated scrape timeout.
- Monitor target endpoint latency.
- Tune scrape timeout only for slow exporters.
- Investigate application-side metrics endpoint performance.
